A. Simplified Estimates using "Rules of Thumb" shown at right
Rules of Thumb for Estimating Oxygen/Air Requirements for Coarse or Fine Bubble Diffusers
i)  INPUTS (Values of "Rule of Thumb" Constants for the Calculations)
1.  The typical AOR/SOR  (or AOTE/SOTE) is 0.50 for a coarse bubble aertion system.
2.  The typical AOR/SOR  (or AOTE/SOTE) is 0.33 for a fine bubble aertion system.
3.  The typical SOTE is 0.75% per foot (2.46%/m) of diffuser submergence for a coarse bubble system
4.  The typical SOTE is 2.0% per foot (6.56%/m) of diffuser submergence for a fine bubble system
5.  Air weighs 1.275 kg/m3 (at 0oC and 1 bar) and contains 23% oxygen by weight, thus:
7.  For biological treatment with SRT from 5 to 10 days, kg oxygen required /kg BOD removed
is typically in the range from 0.92 - 1.1 kg O2/kg BOD.  Higher SRT results in a higher value
of kg O2 required/kg BOD removed.
8.  The oxidation of 1 kg of ammonia nitrogen typically requires 4.1 to 4.6 kg of oxygen.

5. A2O EBPR Process.value: Completely Mixed Activated Sludge Denitrification Calculations - S.I. units
This spreadsheet makes the design calculations for enhanced biological phosphorus removal
with the CMAS system designed for preanoxic denitrification in Worksheet 4.
(The wastewater parameters/characteristics and biological kinetic coefficients entered on
Worksheet 2 will also be used in this worksheet.)
Instructions:  Enter values in blue boxes.  Spreadsheet calculates values in yellow boxes
Equations used for the calculations:  (note that conversion factors are needed in some of the equations)
(target L:W - only used if tank is rectangular)
Calculations - Enhanced Biological Phosporus Removal by A2O Process
NO3-N fed to Anaerobic Tank = RASRatio*Qo*(g rbCOD/d NO3-N)
1.  Calculate the rbCOD available for Enhanced Biological Phosporus Removal
rbCOD consumed by nitrate  = (rbCOD used by nitrate)*(NO3-N fed to Anaerobic Tank)
rbCOD available  = rbCODo - rbCOD consumed by nitrate
P removal rate by EBPR = (rbCOD available)/(rbCOD/P removal)
P Conc. reduction by EBPR = removal rate by EBPR/Qo
3.  Determine P Removal by other heterotrophic bacteria for synthesis
PX,bio = QY(So - S)/(1 + kdSRT)  +  fdkdQY(So - S)SRT/(1 + kdSRT)  +  QYn(NOx)/(1 + kdnSRT)
P removal by Synthesis = 0.015*PX,bio     (in g P/day)
Effluent P Conc., Pe  =  Po - P conc. reduction by EBPR
- reduction of P conc. by other heterotrophic bacteria for synthesis
5.  Calculate the Anoxic Tank Volume, Dimensions and Detention Time
Click on green box and then  on

5. A2O EBPR Process.value: 5.  Calculate P Concentration in Waste Sludge
Tot. Sludge Prod. Rate  =  (PX,bio/0.85) + (Qo*nbVSSo) + Qo*(TSSo - VSSo)
% P in waste sludge =  (P Removal Rate/Total Sludge Prod. Rate)*100%
